title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Dr. Gilchrist meets with Detective Chief Inspector Cloak to provide confidential information regarding Marina Gregg. Gilchrist reveals that following a recent incident, Marina suffered a mental collapse. While he was providing her with sedatives, she told him, "The drink was meant for me, doctor." When Gilchrist later attempted to revisit the topic, Marina denied saying it, claiming he had misunderstood her due to her state of consciousness. Gilchrist notes that while he cannot confirm the truth of her statement, Marina genuinely believed the drug was intended for her, and she has not shared this belief with her husband.

Following this, Blackley Preston leads Demott and Cloak to the second-floor room of Jason Rad. Demott observes that Rad is an unreadable man with a profound intellect and a clown-like face. During an interrogation, Rad explains that he and his wife, Marina, did not know Heather Badcock prior to the incident. Rad claims Heather had only met Marina twelve or thirteen years ago at a garden party in Bermuda, where Heather had requested an autograph. Rad dismisses the idea that Marina was distracted during their recent encounter, suggesting any perceived lapse in attention was merely fatigue.

Cloak confronts Rad with the possibility that the poisoning of Heather Badcock was an accident and that Marina was the intended target. Rad agrees with this assessment, though Cloak notes that Rad had not mentioned this theory to Inspector Cornish or during the inquest.
